sections.label: Section 7.9
sections.original: πόλιν γὰρ φιλόμολπον οἰκεῖ δορικτύπων
sections.translation: For he lives in a city that loves music , the city of the Aeacidae with their clashing spears ;

sections.label: Section 7.10
sections.original: Αἰακιδᾶν · μάλα δʼ ἐθέλοντι σύμπειρον ἀγωνίᾳ θυμὸν ἀμφέπειν .
sections.translation: and they very much want to foster a spirit familiar with contests . If someone is successful in his deeds , he casts a cause for sweet thoughts into the streams of the Muses . For those great acts of prowess dwell in deep darkness , if they lack songs , and we know of only one way to hold a mirror up to fine deeds :

sections.label: Section 7.15
sections.original: εἰ Μναμοσύνας ἕκατι λιπαράμπυκος
sections.translation: if , by the grace of Mnemosyne with her splendid headdress , one finds a recompense for toils in glorious song .

sections.label: Section 7.17
sections.original: σοφοὶ δὲ μέλλοντα τριταῖον ἄνεμον
sections.translation: Skillful men know the wind that will come on the day after tomorrow , and they do not suffer loss through the love of gain . The rich man and the poor man alike travel together to the boundary of death .

sections.label: Section 7.20
sections.original: ἅμα νέονται . ἐγὼ δὲ πλέονʼ ἔλπομαι
sections.translation: And I expect that the story of Odysseus came to exceed his experiences , through the sweet songs of Homer ,

sections.label: Section 7.22
sections.original: ἐπεὶ ψεύδεσί οἱ ποτανᾷ τε μαχανᾷ
sections.translation: since there is a certain solemnity in his lies and winged artfulness , and poetic skill deceives , seducing us with stories , and the heart of the mass of men is blind . For if

sections.label: Section 7.25
sections.original: ἓ τὰν ἀλάθειαν ἰδέμεν , οὔ κεν ὅπλων χολωθεὶς
sections.translation: they had been able to see the truth , then mighty Aias , in anger over the arms , would never have planted in his chest the smooth sword — Aias , who was the most powerful in battle , except for Achilles , and whom the breath of the unswerving Zephyr conveyed in swift ships , to bring back the wife of golden-haired Menelaus

sections.label: Section 7.30
sections.original: πρὸς Ἴλου πόλιν . ἀλλὰ κοινὸν γὰρ ἔρχεται
sections.translation: from the city of Ilus . But the wave of Hades breaks over all alike ; it falls on the man who does not expect it , and on the one who does . There is honor for those whose fame a god causes to grow luxuriant when they are dead . Neoptolemus came to help , [*] to the great navel of the broad-bosomed earth . And he lies beneath the Pythian soil ,

sections.label: Section 8.1
sections.original: ὥρα πότνια , κάρυξ Ἀφροδίτας ἀμβροσιᾶν φιλοτάτων ,
sections.translation: Queenly Season of Youth , herald of the divine embraces of Aphrodite , you who rest in the eyes of young girls and boys , and carry one man in the gentle arms of compulsion , but handle another man differently . It is a desirable thing , for one who has not strayed from due measure in any deed ,

sections.label: Section 8.5
sections.original: τῶν ἀρειόνων ἐρώτων ἐπικρατεῖν δύνασθαι .
sections.translation: to be able to win the better kinds of love ;

sections.label: Section 8.6
sections.original: 10 οἷοι καὶ Διὸς Αἰγίνας τε λέκτρον ποιμένες ἀμφεπόλησαν
sections.translation: such loves , the shepherds of Cyprian Aphrodite ’ s gifts , attended the marriage-bed of Zeus and Aegina . And from that union a son was born , the king of Oenone , the best in hands and mind . Many men often prayed that they might see him ; for , unbidden , the choicest heroes that dwelled around him

sections.label: Section 8.10
sections.original: ἤθελον κείνου γε πείθεσθʼ ἀναξίαις ἑκόντες ,
sections.translation: wanted to submit to his commands willingly ,

sections.label: Section 8.11
sections.original: 20 οἵ τε κρανααῖς ἐν Ἀθάναισιν ἅρμοζον στρατόν ,
sections.translation: those who marshalled their people in rocky Athens , and the descendants of Pelops in Sparta . As a suppliant I cling to the sacred knees of Aeacus , on behalf of his dear city and these citizens , bringing

sections.label: Section 8.15
sections.original: Λυδίαν μίτραν καναχηδὰ πεποικιλμέναν ,
sections.translation: a Lydian crown embroidered with song , glory from Nemea in the double foot race for Deinias and his father Megas . For prosperity that is planted with a god ’ s blessing is more abiding for men ;

sections.label: Section 8.18
sections.original: 30 ὅσπερ καὶ Κινύραν ἔβρισε πλούτῳ ποντίᾳ ἔν ποτε Κύπρῳ .
sections.translation: such prosperity as once loaded Cinyras with wealth in sea-washed Cyprus . I stand with feet lightly poised , catching my breath before I speak .

sections.label: Section 8.20
sections.original: πολλὰ γὰρ πολλᾷ λέλεκται · ν εα ρὰ δʼ ἐξευρόντα δόμεν βασάνῳ
sections.translation: For many stories have been told in many ways . But to find something new and submit it to the touchstone for testing is danger itself . Words are a dainty morsel for the envious ; and envy always clings to the noble , and has no quarrel with worse men .

sections.label: Section 8.23
sections.original: 40 κεῖνος καὶ Τελαμῶνος δάψεν υἱὸν φασγάνῳ ἀμφικυλίσαις .
sections.translation: Envy devoured the son of Telamon , throwing him onto his own sword . A man who was not gifted in speech , but brave in his heart , is held down by oblivion

sections.label: Section 8.25
sections.original: ἐν λυγρῷ νείκει · μέγιστον δʼ αἰόλῳ ψεύδει γέρας ἀντέταται .
sections.translation: under deadly strife ; and the greatest prize of honor has been offered to the shifty lie . For in a secret vote the Danaans favored Odysseus ; and Aias , robbed of the golden armor , wrestled with death .

sections.label: Section 8.28
sections.original: ἦ μὰν ἀνόμοιά γε δᾴοισιν ἐν θερμῷ χροῒ
sections.translation: Truly , they did not tear equal wounds in the warm flesh of the enemy when they were driven back

sections.label: Section 8.30
sections.original: ὑπʼ ἀλεξιμβρότῳ λόγχᾳ , τὰ μὲν ἀμφʼ Ἀχιλεῖ νεοκτόνῳ ,
sections.translation: under the man-protecting spear , at one time around the newly-slain corpse of Achilles , and on destructive days spent on other toils . It seems that hateful Misrepresentation existed even long ago : a fellow traveler of flattering tales , deceitful-minded , a malignant disgrace . She does violence to the illustrious , and upholds the rotten glory of the obscure .

sections.label: Section 9.1
sections.original: κωμάσομεν παρʼ Ἀπόλλωνος Σικυώνοθε , Μοῖσαι ,
sections.translation: Muses , we will go in victory procession from Apollo ’ s shrine in Sicyon to newly-founded Aetna , where the doors flung open wide are overwhelmed by guests , at the prosperous home of Chromius . Make a sweet song of verses ! For , mounting his chariot of victorious horses , he gives the word to sing for the mother and her twin children

sections.label: Section 9.5
sections.original: Πυθῶνος αἰπεινᾶς ὁμοκλάροις ἐπόπταις .
sections.translation: who jointly watch over steep Pytho .

sections.label: Section 9.6
sections.original: ἔστι δέ τις λόγος ἀνθρώπων , τετελεσμένον ἐσλὸν
sections.translation: There is a saying among men : a noble deed when it is accomplished should not be buried silently in the ground ; and divine song is suited to boasting . But we will wake the shouting lyre and the flute in honor of the very pinnacle of horse-contests , which Adrastus established for Phoebus by the streams of the Asopus .

sections.label: Section 9.10
sections.original: μνασθεὶς ἐπασκήσω κλυταῖς ἥρωα τιμαῖς ,
sections.translation: Having mentioned them , I will adorn that hero with glorious honors ,

sections.label: Section 9.11
sections.original: ὃς τότε μὲν βασιλεύων κεῖθι νέαισί θʼ ἑορταῖς
sections.translation: who , at the time when he was ruler there , made his city famous and glorious with new festivals , and contests of men ’ s strength , and hollow chariots . For once Adrastus fled from bold-thinking Amphiaraus and terrible civil strife , from his ancestral home , Argos ; and the sons of Talaus were no longer rulers , overpowered by sedition .

sections.label: Section 9.15
sections.original: κρέσσων δὲ καππαύει δίκαν τὰν πρόσθεν ἀνήρ .
sections.translation: A stronger man puts an end to the previous justice .

sections.label: Section 9.16
sections.original: ἀνδροδάμαντʼ Ἐριφύλαν , ὅρκιον ὡς ὅτε πιστόν ,
sections.translation: The sons of Talaus gave man-conquering Eriphyle , as a faithful pledge , in marriage to Amphiaraus son of Oicles , and became the most powerful of the golden-haired Danaans ; and once they led a noble army of men to seven-gated Thebes — an expedition not attended by birds of good omen . In their mad desire to leave home , the son of Cronus , by whirling his lightning-bolt , urged them not to go ,

sections.label: Section 9.20
sections.original: στείχειν ἐπώτρυνʼ , ἀλλὰ φείσασθαι κελεύθου .
sections.translation: but to abstain from the journey .

sections.label: Section 9.21
sections.original: 50 φαινομέναν δʼ ἄρʼ ἐς ἄταν σπεῦδεν ὅμιλος ἱκέσθαι
sections.translation: And so that company , in bronze armor , and with their horses in war-harnesses , was hastening to arrive at manifest doom . And planting [*] their sweet return on the banks of the Ismenus , they fattened the white-flowering smoke with their corpses [*] . For seven funeral pyres feasted on their bodies ’ young limbs . But , for the sake of Amphiaraus , Zeus with his all-powerful thunderbolt

sections.label: Section 9.25
sections.original: 60 Ζεὺς τὰν βαθύστερνον χθόνα , κρύψεν δʼ ἅμʼ ἵπποις ,
sections.translation: split the deep-breasted earth , and concealed him together with his horses ,

sections.label: Section 9.26
sections.original: δουρὶ Περικλυμένου πρὶν νῶτα τυπέντα μαχατὰν
sections.translation: before he could be struck in the back by the spear of Periclymenus , and his warlike spirit disgraced . For amid divinely-sent panic even the children of gods flee . If it is possible , son of Cronus , I would like to put off for as long as I can this fierce trial of empurpled spears , this contest for life and death ;

sections.label: Section 9.30
sections.original: αἰτέω σε παισὶν δαρὸν Αἰτναίων ὀπάζειν ,
sections.translation: and I pray to you , grant to the children of the Aetnaeans the long-lived blessing of good laws ,

sections.label: Section 10.1
sections.original: Δαναοῦ πόλιν ἀγλαοθρόνων τε πεντήκοντα κορᾶν , Χάριτες ,
sections.translation: Graces , sing of the city of Danaus and his fifty daughters on their splendid thrones , Hera ’ s Argos , a home suitable for a god ; it blazes with countless excellences because of its bold deeds . Long indeed is the story of Perseus and the Gorgon Medusa ,

sections.label: Section 10.5
sections.original: πολλὰ δʼ Αἰγύπτῳ καταοίκισθεν ἄστη ταῖς Ἐπάφου παλάμαις ·
sections.translation: and many are the cities founded in Egypt by the devising of Epaphus . Nor did Hypermnestra go astray , when she restrained in its scabbard her sword , which was alone in its verdict .

sections.label: Section 10.7
sections.original: Διομήδεα δʼ ἄμβροτον ξανθά ποτε Γλαυκῶπις ἔθηκε θεόν ·
sections.translation: And once the golden-haired , gray-eyed goddess made Diomedes an immortal god ; and the earth in Thebes , thunder-struck by the bolts of Zeus , swallowed up the prophetic son of Oicles , Amphiaraus , the storm-cloud of war .

sections.label: Section 10.10
sections.original: καὶ γυναιξὶν καλλικόμοισιν ἀριστεύει πάλαι ·
sections.translation: And Argos has long been the best city for women with beautiful hair ; Zeus made this saying clear by visiting Alcmena and Danae , and he united the fruit of intelligence with straightforward justice in the father of Adrastus and in Lynceus .

sections.label: Section 10.13
sections.original: θρέψε δʼ αἰχμὰν Ἀμφιτρύωνος . ὁ δʼ ὄλβῳ φέρτατος
sections.translation: And Zeus nourished the spear of Amphitryon , who attained the height of prosperity and entered into kinship with that god , when , clad in bronze armor ,

sections.label: Section 10.15
sections.original: Τηλεβόας ἔναρεν · τῷ ὄψιν ἐειδόμενος
sections.translation: he slew the Teleboae . Taking on the appearance of Amphitryon , the king of the immortals entered the hall of that hero , bearing the fearless seed of Heracles ; whose bride Hebe , the most beautiful of the goddesses , walks forever in Olympus beside her mother Hera , goddess of marriage .

sections.label: Section 10.19
sections.original: βραχύ μοι στόμα πάντʼ ἀναγήσασθʼ , ὅσων Ἀργεῖον ἔχει τέμενος
sections.translation: My mouth is too small to tell the whole story of all the noble things in which the precinct of Argos has a share .

sections.label: Section 10.20
sections.original: μοῖραν ἐσλῶν · ἔστι δὲ καὶ κόρος ἀνθρώπων βαρὺς ἀντιάσαι ·
sections.translation: And there is also the satiety of men , which is grievous to encounter . But nevertheless , awaken the well-strung lyre , and take thought of wrestling ; the contest for the bronze shield calls the people to the sacrifice of oxen in honor of Hera and to the trial of contests . There the son of Ulias , Theaeus , was victorious twice , and gained forgetfulness of toils that were bravely borne .

sections.label: Section 10.25
sections.original: ἐκράτησε δὲ καί ποθʼ Ἕλλανα στρατὸν Πυθῶνι , τύχᾳ τε μολὼν
sections.translation: And he once was victor over the people of Greece at Pytho ; and , going with good fortune , he won the crown at the Isthmus and at Nemea , and he gave the Muses a field to plough , since he won three times at the gates of the sea , and three times on the sacred ground , according to the ordinance of Adrastus . Father Zeus , his mouth keeps silent what his heart truly desires . The accomplishment of all

sections.label: Section 10.30
sections.original: ἐν τὶν ἔργων · οὐδʼ , ἀμόχθῳ καρδίᾳ προσφέρων τόλμαν , παραιτεῖται χάριν ·
sections.translation: deeds rests with you . Adding boldness to a heart that does not shrink from labor [*] , he asks for your grace .

sections.label: Section 11.1
sections.original: παῖ Ῥέας , ἅ τε πρυτανεῖα λέλογχας , Ἑστία ,
sections.translation: Daughter of Rhea , you who have received the town hall under your protection , Hestia , sister of Zeus the highest and of Hera who shares his throne , welcome Aristagoras to your dwelling , and welcome to a place near your splendid scepter his companions ,

sections.label: Section 11.5
sections.original: οἵ σε γεραίροντες ὀρθὰν φυλάσσοισιν Τένεδον ,
sections.translation: who , in honoring you , guard Tenedos and keep her on a straight course ;

sections.label: Section 11.6
sections.original: πολλὰ μὲν λοιβαῖσιν ἀγαζόμενοι πρώταν θεῶν ,
sections.translation: often they worship you , first of the gods , with libations , and often with the savor of burnt sacrifice . Lyres and songs peal among them , and Themis , who belongs to Zeus the god of hospitality , is honored with everlasting feasts . With glory to the end

sections.label: Section 11.10
sections.original: δωδεκάμηνον περᾶσαι σὺν ἀτρώτῳ κραδίᾳ ,
sections.translation: may he fulfill his twelve-month office , with his heart unwounded .

sections.label: Section 11.11
sections.original: ἄνδρα δʼ ἐγὼ μακαρίζω μὲν πατέρʼ Ἁγησίλαν ,
sections.translation: I call that man blessed in his father Hagesilas , in his marvellous body , and in his inborn steadiness . But if any man who has prosperity surpasses others in beauty , and displays his strength by being best in the games ,

sections.label: Section 11.15
sections.original: 20 θνατὰ μεμνάσθω περιστέλλων μέλη ,
sections.translation: let him remember that his robes are thrown around mortal limbs , and that he will clothe himself with earth , the end of all .

sections.label: Section 11.17
sections.original: ἐν λόγοις δʼ ἀστῶν ἀγαθοῖσί νιν αἰνεῖσθαι χρεών ,
sections.translation: Yet it is right for him to be praised in the good words of his fellow-citizens , and for us to adorn him with the honeyed sound of songs . For in contests of those who live around him , sixteen

sections.label: Section 11.20
sections.original: ἀγλααὶ νῖκαι πάτραν τʼ εὐώνυμον
sections.translation: splendid victories crowned Aristagoras and his illustrious fatherland , in wrestling and in the proud pancratium .

sections.label: Section 11.22
sections.original: ἐλπίδες δʼ ὀκνηρότεραι γονέων παιδὸς βίαν
sections.translation: But the too hesitant hopes of his parents restrained the boy ’ s strength from attempting the contests at Pytho and Olympia . For I swear by the power of Oath : in my judgment , whether he went to Castalia

sections.label: Section 11.25
sections.original: καὶ παρʼ εὐδένδρῳ μολὼν ὄχθῳ Κρόνου
sections.translation: or to the well-wooded hill of Cronus , he would have returned home in finer fashion than the opponents who strove against him ,

sections.label: Section 11.27
sections.original: πενταετηρίδʼ ἑορτὰν Ἡρακλέος τέθμιον
sections.translation: having celebrated the four years ’ festival ordained by Heracles , and having crowned his hair with purple wreaths . But , among mortals , empty-headed pride

sections.label: Section 11.30
sections.original: ἐξ ἀγαθῶν ἔβαλον · τὸν δʼ αὖ καταμεμφθέντʼ ἄγαν
sections.translation: casts one man out of his goods ; and a timid spirit foils another man of the fine achievements that should be his , dragging him back by the hand as he disparages his own strength too much .
